notes:
**Fixes & polish**

- **The Glass theme now actually shows your desktop.** On macOS, the sidebars (and the reading surface, if you enable it) become genuinely translucent over your wallpaper — the vibrancy was being blocked by an opaque background layer painted over the transparent window, and by the transparent-window setting not reaching the packaged build. Both are fixed. Text stays fully opaque, as before. (PRD Addendum 24 §5, Update 1.)
- **The theme picker cards line up.** The Glass and High Contrast cards no longer sit taller or misaligned; every theme card shares the same size and its name shows in full, with the platform/AAA badge tucked into the preview.
- **Type sizes follow the design scale.** The Appearance panel's titles, labels, and hints now use their correct roles from the type scale instead of all sitting at one size.

**New**

- **Refined theme palettes.** Every theme now has a distinct chrome tone for the sidebars — a mid-tone step away from the reading surface (lighter rails in dark mode, per the macOS convention) — so the app reads with more depth. Aurora's artwork is bolder at the edges and calmer under your text, and it now joins the main row of the theme picker. (Theming Update 1: base2 · card-lift · Aurora v2.)
- **Mention cards lift instead of casting shadows.** Linked-mention cards are now the lightest surface in the window rather than a shadowed box — Infiniti expresses depth through tone, never drop shadows. (Update 1 §T26/T27.)
